_comment: === BENCHMARK v2 — Curated, tight chunk sets ===
_instructions: Each question has 3-8 core chunks only — the ones that directly define or explain the concept.
Intro/transition/wrap-up chunks are excluded to avoid inflating Recall and MRR.
Negative questions have empty expected_chunk_indices by design.
This benchmark is suitable for Phase 1 validation and regression testing into Phase 2.

question: What is HNSW?
notes: HNSW starts at chunk 118 (42:45) — instructor introduces the graph concept, connects nearest nodes, and builds layers. Chunks 119-122 cover node connections and nearest-neighbor graph building. Chunks 124, 126, 127 cover layer promotion and multi-layer structure. Chunks 206-207 contain the explicit final comparison where HNSW is named. BSP/KD-tree chunks (74-117) are intentionally excluded — different algorithm.

question: Explain IVF.
notes: IVF core: chunk 43 introduces आईबीएफ/Inverted File Index. Chunks 44-50 cover centroid selection, cluster assignment, and searching only the nearest cluster. Chunks 200-202 cover IVF+PQ combination. Chunks 51-62 are the extended worked example — excluded to keep the set tight.

question: What is Product Quantization?
notes: PQ core starts at chunk 157 (57:14) — 'compress this vector'. Chunks 158-167 cover segment splitting, codebook construction, and index assignment. Chunk 196 has the key memory savings calculation (6144 bytes → 12 bytes). Chunks 168-195 are the detailed distance-table lookup walkthrough — important but not the definition, so excluded from core set.

question: How does vector search work?
notes: Core vector search explanation: chunk 4 introduces distance between vectors, chunks 5 and 15-20 cover finding nearest neighbors via Euclidean distance and brute-force scan across all items. Chunks 2-3 are intro/framing, chunks 21-27 are Euclidean formula derivation — all excluded.

question: What is a vector database?
notes: Chunk 7 (02:30) introduces vector database concept. Chunks 8-10 cover internal architecture and why older databases were not used. Chunks 209-211 list real vector database products (Milvus, pgvector, etc.). Chunk 11 is a design-level question, not a definition — excluded.

question: Explain approximate nearest neighbor search.
notes: ANN introduced at chunk 29 (10:49) contrasting with exact search. Chunks 30-35 cover the accuracy vs speed trade-off and the motivation for ANN. This is a tight, self-contained section.

question: What is cosine similarity?
notes: Cosine similarity is briefly mentioned at chunks 22-23 (08:14-09:02) as an alternative to Euclidean distance. It is not deeply explained in this video — expect lower Recall here. This is intentional: a good retriever should still find these two chunks.

question: What is the difference between IVF and HNSW?
notes: Chunk 206 (1:15:55) is the direct comparison: HNSW (high speed, high accuracy, high memory) vs IVF (lower accuracy, low memory). Chunks 207-208 complete the comparison table. Chunks 43 and 118 are the IVF and HNSW definition entry points — included so a retriever that finds the definitions also scores well on this comparison query.

question: What is Kubernetes?
notes: NEGATIVE — Kubernetes is not discussed in this video. Retriever should return irrelevant or low-confidence results. Used to test precision.

question: Explain Quantum Computing.
notes: NEGATIVE — Quantum Computing is not discussed in this video. Used to test precision.
